vocabulary.meaning: cut something into pieces with a sharp tool such as a knife
vocabulary.example: Chop the onions into small pieces.

vocabulary.meaning: cut something into thin, flat pieces
vocabulary.example: Can you slice the bread for me?

vocabulary.meaning: rub food against a grater in order to cut it into small pieces
vocabulary.example: She grated some cheese over the pasta.

vocabulary.meaning: cook food in an oven without extra fat or liquid
vocabulary.example: I love the smell of baking bread.

vocabulary.meaning: cook food under or over direct heat
vocabulary.example: We grilled some fish on the barbecue.

vocabulary.meaning: cook food in hot fat or oil
vocabulary.example: Fry the onions in a little olive oil.

vocabulary.meaning: cook meat or vegetables in an oven or over a fire
vocabulary.example: We had roast chicken for Sunday lunch.

vocabulary.meaning: cook food in water that is boiling
vocabulary.example: Boil the potatoes for about twenty minutes.

vocabulary.meaning: someone who prepares and cooks food
vocabulary.example: My mother is a very good cook.

vocabulary.meaning: a large piece of kitchen equipment used for cooking food
vocabulary.example: We bought a new gas cooker yesterday.

vocabulary.meaning: a professional cook, especially the chief cook in a restaurant
vocabulary.example: He works as a chef in a French restaurant.

vocabulary.meaning: a piece of kitchen equipment which is used for baking or heating food
vocabulary.example: Put the pizza in the hot oven.

vocabulary.meaning: the part of a cooker where food is cooked under direct heat
vocabulary.example: Put the sausages under the grill.

vocabulary.meaning: the top part of a cooker where pans are heated
vocabulary.example: Don't touch the hob while it's still hot.

vocabulary.meaning: a room where food is prepared and cooked
vocabulary.example: She was cooking dinner in the kitchen.

vocabulary.meaning: a style or method of cooking, especially of a particular country or region
vocabulary.example: I really enjoy Italian cuisine.

vocabulary.meaning: a meal eaten in the middle of the day
vocabulary.example: What did you have for lunch?

vocabulary.meaning: the main meal of the day, eaten either in the middle of the day or in the evening
vocabulary.example: We had dinner at a restaurant last night.

vocabulary.meaning: a flat, round dish that you put food on
vocabulary.example: He piled his plate high with food.

vocabulary.meaning: a round, deep dish used for holding food or liquid
vocabulary.example: She ate a bowl of soup for lunch.

vocabulary.meaning: a small shallow dish on which a cup stands
vocabulary.example: She placed the cup carefully on the saucer.

vocabulary.meaning: a container for cooking or serving food, or food prepared as part of a meal
vocabulary.example: Lasagna is a very popular Italian dish.

vocabulary.meaning: a plant or part of a plant that is eaten as food
vocabulary.example: You should eat more fresh vegetables.

vocabulary.meaning: someone who does not eat meat or fish
vocabulary.example: She became a vegetarian when she was a teenager.

vocabulary.meaning: someone who does not eat or use any animal products
vocabulary.example: Strict vegans do not eat honey or wear leather.

vocabulary.meaning: hot food that is served very quickly in special restaurants
vocabulary.example: Eating too much fast food is unhealthy.

vocabulary.meaning: a meal that you buy in a restaurant and eat somewhere else
vocabulary.example: We decided to get a Chinese takeaway tonight.

vocabulary.meaning: a container with a lid, handle, and spout, used for boiling water
vocabulary.example: The kettle is boiling, so I'll make tea.

vocabulary.meaning: a container in which tea is made and from which it is poured
vocabulary.example: She filled the teapot with boiling water.

vocabulary.meaning: a large piece of electrical equipment that is used to keep food frozen
vocabulary.example: We store frozen meat in the freezer.

vocabulary.meaning: a large piece of electrical equipment that is used to keep food cold
vocabulary.example: Keep the milk in the fridge.

vocabulary.meaning: preserved by freezing
vocabulary.example: I bought some frozen vegetables.

vocabulary.meaning: very cold, or at the temperature when water turns to ice
vocabulary.example: The weather outside is absolutely freezing.

vocabulary.meaning: combine two or more substances together
vocabulary.example: Mix the flour and sugar in a large bowl.

vocabulary.meaning: move a liquid or substance around with a spoon or stick
vocabulary.example: Stir the soup occasionally so it doesn't burn.

vocabulary.meaning: beat eggs, cream, etc. with a whisk to incorporate air
vocabulary.example: Whisk the egg whites until they are stiff.

vocabulary.meaning: a cold drink that does not contain alcohol
vocabulary.example: Cola and lemonade are soft drinks.

vocabulary.meaning: a sweet drink that contains bubbles of gas
vocabulary.example: I don't like fizzy drinks because they have too much sugar.

vocabulary.meaning: a list of the food and drinks available in a restaurant
vocabulary.example: Can we see the menu, please?

vocabulary.meaning: a complete list of items, usually in alphabetical or systematic order
vocabulary.example: She looked through the clothing catalogue.

vocabulary.meaning: small cubes of sweet gelatin dessert
vocabulary.example: She added jelly cubes to the fruit salad.

vocabulary.meaning: a deep round metal cooking pot with one long handle and a lid
vocabulary.example: Fill the saucepan with water and add some salt.

vocabulary.meaning: free from ice, or unfreeze food
vocabulary.example: Defrost the chicken before cooking it.

vocabulary.meaning: a small hot-tasting pod of a variety of capsicum
vocabulary.example: Add some chili to make the curry spicier.

vocabulary.meaning: a writer who analyzes and reviews food and restaurants
vocabulary.example: The food critic gave the new bistro a five-star review.

vocabulary.meaning: a single period or step in a process
vocabulary.example: At one stage, we thought we would have to cancel the party.

vocabulary.meaning: by the way, or as an added point
vocabulary.example: Incidentally, did you remember to buy the milk?

vocabulary.meaning: a person or thing competing with another for the same objective
vocabulary.example: The two restaurants are fierce rivals.

vocabulary.meaning: a writer and performer of songs in the Middle Ages, or a specific type of dish
vocabulary.example: The troubadour performed songs at the medieval banquet.

vocabulary.meaning: a small dish served before the main meal
vocabulary.example: I'll have the soup as a starter.

vocabulary.meaning: not fit to be eaten
vocabulary.example: The burnt meat was completely inedible.

vocabulary.meaning: a specific dish name
vocabulary.example: We ordered the Sentinale dish from the menu.

vocabulary.meaning: ceramic material that can withstand high temperatures
vocabulary.example: The baking dish is made of heat-resistant ceramic.

vocabulary.meaning: thick and juicy (used to describe food)
vocabulary.example: The steak was chunky and succulent.

vocabulary.meaning: a round patty of minced beef, fried or grilled and typically served in a bun
vocabulary.example: He ordered a double beef-burger with cheese.

vocabulary.meaning: a little bit, or to a small degree
vocabulary.example: The soup is slightly too salty for my taste.

vocabulary.meaning: moderately, or to a reasonable degree
vocabulary.example: The restaurant was fairly crowded on Friday night.

vocabulary.meaning: knives, forks, and spoons used for eating
vocabulary.example: Please lay the cutlery on the table.

vocabulary.meaning: an object for holding or transporting something
vocabulary.example: Pack the leftovers in a plastic container.

vocabulary.meaning: a person who studies or is an expert in nutrition
vocabulary.example: The nutritionist advised us to eat more vegetables.

vocabulary.meaning: easily broken or damaged, or subtle
vocabulary.example: These wine glasses are very delicate, so wash them carefully.

vocabulary.meaning: a light evening meal
vocabulary.example: We had a simple supper of bread and cheese.

vocabulary.meaning: all of the people born and living at about the same time
vocabulary.example: This recipe has been passed down through generations.

vocabulary.meaning: the distinctive taste of a food or drink
vocabulary.example: This coffee has a rich, bold flavour.

vocabulary.meaning: a small Greek restaurant or tavern
vocabulary.example: We had dinner at a cozy little taverna by the harbor.

vocabulary.meaning: insensitive to criticism or insults
vocabulary.example: You need to be thick-skinned to work as a food critic.

vocabulary.meaning: a work of outstanding artistry, skill, or culinary creation
vocabulary.example: The chef's signature dessert is a culinary masterpiece.

phrasalVerbs.meaning: make a short, informal visit without booking
phrasalVerbs.example: EX: Drop in on us whenever you are in the neighborhood.

phrasalVerbs.meaning: be near a particular age, time, or number
phrasalVerbs.example: EX: It must be getting on for midnight.

phrasalVerbs.meaning: become rotten or bad to eat
phrasalVerbs.example: EX: The milk has gone off.
EX: She went on talking even though nobody was listening.
EX: I think I'll go round and see how he is.
EX: Why don't you come round for dinner tonight?
EX: He kept on asking me questions all afternoon.
EX: You can leave out the next exercise if you want.

phrasalVerbs.meaning: make someone dislike something or not want to do something
phrasalVerbs.example: EX: The smell of the food put me off.
EX: I ran into an old friend at the supermarket yesterday.

phrasalVerbs.meaning: use all of something so that you have none left
phrasalVerbs.example: EX: We've run out of sugar, so I need to buy some.

phrasalVerbs.meaning: start to like someone or something, or start doing something regularly
phrasalVerbs.example: EX: He took to golf as soon as he retired.

phrasalVerbs.meaning: test something to see if it is good or works well
phrasalVerbs.example: EX: I'd like to try out this new recipe.

phrasalVerbs.meaning: happen in a particular way or have a particular result
phrasalVerbs.example: EX: The cake turned out perfectly in the end.

phrasalVerbs.meaning: arrive unexpectedly or after a delay
phrasalVerbs.example: EX: He turned up at the party without an invitation.

collocations.meaning: a person who is very good at preparing and cooking food
prepare and heat food for eating
prepare and cook meals as a regular activity
prepare a beverage for someone
consume a portion of a liquid
swallow a liquid
raise a glass and drink to honor someone
raise a glass to wish someone good health
raise a glass together to honor someone
give food to a domestic or wild animal
consume a particular food (typically used for animals)
make a container completely full of something
containing as much of something as possible
prepare or offer meals
quick, unhealthy food
food specially prepared for domestic pets
food that is believed to be very good for your health
prepare or consume a meal
eat at a restaurant
write something down so that you remember it
quickly write something down in a notebook
at a particular time when something happens
sometimes, but not very often
an important event or celebration
cook food by following a set of instructions
a book containing recipes and cooking instructions
a situation that is very likely to have a bad result
arrange dishes and cutlery on a table, or remove them after a meal
arrange to have a table in a restaurant at a particular time
clean plates, bowls, and cutlery after a meal
clean one's hands with water and soap
wash the dishes after a meal
household appliances for washing dishes or clothes

wordPatterns.meaning: connect something or someone with something else in your mind
wordPatterns.example: EX: Most people associate fast food with poor health.

wordPatterns.meaning: giving serious attention to what you are doing to avoid mistakes
wordPatterns.example: EX: Be careful with that hot saucepan.

wordPatterns.meaning: make a choice between two possibilities
wordPatterns.example: EX: We had to choose between fish and chicken.

wordPatterns.meaning: decide to perform a certain action
wordPatterns.example: EX: Many people choose to become vegetarian.

wordPatterns.meaning: say something nice to someone about their achievement or appearance
wordPatterns.example: EX: The guests complimented the chef on the excellent meal.

wordPatterns.meaning: containing a lot of something
wordPatterns.example: EX: The teapot was full of freshly brewed tea.

wordPatterns.meaning: do not have enough of something
wordPatterns.example: EX: The dish lacks salt and spices.

wordPatterns.meaning: the state of not having enough of something
wordPatterns.example: EX: The failure of the cake was due to a lack of eggs.

wordPatterns.meaning: not having enough of a particular quality
wordPatterns.example: EX: His cooking is lacking in flavor.

wordPatterns.meaning: present something to someone for them to accept or reject
wordPatterns.example: EX: She offered the guests some soft drinks.

wordPatterns.meaning: present something to someone
wordPatterns.example: EX: We offered some of our takeaway to our neighbors.

wordPatterns.meaning: volunteer to do something for someone
wordPatterns.example: EX: He offered to do the washing-up after dinner.

wordPatterns.meaning: consider someone to be a particular thing
wordPatterns.example: EX: She is regarded as one of the best cooks in the country.

wordPatterns.meaning: keep in mind to perform a future action
wordPatterns.example: EX: Please remember to turn off the oven when you're done.

wordPatterns.meaning: recall a past event, person, or action
wordPatterns.example: EX: I remember tasting this dish in Rome.

wordPatterns.meaning: keep in mind a particular fact
wordPatterns.example: EX: Remember that the milk goes off quickly in hot weather.

wordPatterns.meaning: propose an idea or action to someone
wordPatterns.example: EX: She suggested getting a Chinese takeaway to her friends.

wordPatterns.meaning: propose that a particular action should be done
wordPatterns.example: EX: The nutritionist suggested that we eat less fast food.

wordPatterns.meaning: be likely to behave in a particular way or have a particular characteristic
wordPatterns.example: EX: Children tend to prefer sweet foods.

wordPatterns.meaning: stay in a place or delay action until something happens
wordPatterns.example: EX: We are waiting for the kettle to boil.

wordPatterns.meaning: delay action or judgment until you see how a situation develops
wordPatterns.example: EX: We'll have to wait and see how the cake turns out.

wordPatterns.meaning: ready, eager, or prepared to do something
wordPatterns.example: EX: He is willing to help us prepare the buffet.

wordFormation.meaning: anxious: feeling worried or nervous / anxiety: a feeling of worry or nervousness
wordFormation.example:
EX: She was anxious about the exam results.
EX: Deep breathing can help reduce anxiety.

wordFormation.meaning: appreciate: recognize the full value of something / appreciation: recognition of value or gratitude / appreciative: showing appreciation
wordFormation.example:
EX: We appreciate your help with the party.
EX: He showed his appreciation by bringing flowers.
EX: The audience was very appreciative of the performance.

wordFormation.meaning: contain: hold or have within / container: an object that can be used to hold things / content: happy or satisfied (adj) or things held inside (n)
wordFormation.example:
EX: This bottle contains mineral water.
EX: Keep the food in an airtight container.
EX: He was content with his simple life.
EX: The contents of the box were damaged.

wordFormation.meaning: creator: a person who creates something / creativity: the use of imagination to create / creative: having or showing imagination
wordFormation.example:
EX: He is the creator of this famous recipe.
EX: Children have a lot of natural creativity.
EX: Cooking is a highly creative activity.

wordFormation.meaning: disgust: make someone feel strong dislike or disapproval / disgusting: extremely unpleasant or unacceptable / disgusted: feeling strong disapproval or dislike
wordFormation.example:
EX: The smell of rotten meat disgusted everyone.
EX: The food at that cheap restaurant was disgusting.
EX: She was disgusted by their rude behavior.

wordFormation.meaning: mixture: a combination of different things / mixer: an electrical device for mixing things / mixed: containing a variety of different things
wordFormation.example:
EX: Pour the mixture into the baking tin.
EX: She used an electric mixer to whisk the cream.
EX: He has mixed feelings about moving abroad.

wordFormation.meaning: growth: the process of increasing in size / grown-up: an adult / growing: increasing in size or amount / home-grown: grown in one's own garden or country / grower: a person who grows crops
wordFormation.example:
EX: The business has seen rapid growth this year.
EX: He behaves like a child, but he's a grown-up.
EX: Organic farming is a growing trend.
EX: I love home-grown tomatoes.
EX: He is a local apple grower.

wordFormation.meaning: origin: the point where something begins / original: first or earliest / originate: have a specified beginning
wordFormation.example:
EX: The origin of this tradition is unknown.
EX: The original plan was to go by train.
EX: The game originated in ancient Egypt.

wordFormation.meaning: preparation: the act of preparing / preparatory: done in order to prepare for something / unprepared: not ready
wordFormation.example:
EX: We need a lot of preparation for the wedding.
EX: Students took preparatory courses before the exam.
EX: He was unprepared for the difficult questions.

wordFormation.meaning: safe: protected from danger / safety: the state of being safe / saviour: a person who saves someone from danger / saver: a person who saves money or energy / save: rescue from danger or store / safely: in a safe way
wordFormation.example:
EX: It is not safe to walk alone at night.
EX: Safety should be our main priority.
EX: The search party was hailed as their saviours.
EX: He is a big saver who spends very little.
EX: The firefighter saved the child.
EX: The plane landed safely at the airport.

wordFormation.meaning: surprise: an unexpected event / surprising: causing surprise / surprised: feeling surprise / surprisingly: in a surprising way
wordFormation.example:
EX: His visit was a pleasant surprise.
EX: It was surprising that he passed the test.
EX: She was surprised to see him.
EX: Surprisingly, the food was quite good.

wordFormation.meaning: sweet: tasting of sugar / sweetener: a substance used to sweeten food / sweetness: the quality of tasting sweet
wordFormation.example:
EX: She likes sweet tea.
EX: He uses artificial sweetener instead of sugar.
EX: The sweetness of the fruit was perfect.

wordFormation.meaning: thorough: complete and detailed / thoroughness: the quality of being thorough / thoroughly: completely and detail-oriented
wordFormation.example:
EX: The police did a thorough search of the house.
EX: She is known for her thoroughness in her work.
EX: Wash your hands thoroughly with soap.
